## What Happened

Prevented misdiagnosis of validation failure. Without the flag, might have blamed sample size (n=24) or data quality instead of recognizing that per-article and outlet-level constructs are incompatible.

## The Mechanism

Explicit surfacing of construct mismatch: TQ (article-level transparency/quality) does not map onto outlet-level reliability (MBFC's reliability_label). User flagged this validity threat when re-validation showed r=-0.094, p=0.663.

## Why It Matters

When validation fails with weak results, the first question should be 'do the constructs actually measure the same thing?' This explicit flag prevents methodological dead-ends disguised as sample-size problems.

## Counterfactual

Without this trigger: User could have spent iterations trying to enlarge sample or collect better-quality data, never noticing that the constructs don't match. This would have been wasted effort on a category error.

## Implications

[broader pattern — other situations this applies to]
